nodes.name: 棕尾鵟
nodes.summary: 棕尾鵟（学名：Buteo rufinus）为鹰科鵟属的鸟类。多栖息于开阔的平原和草原。该物种的模式产地在苏丹。 保护 中国国家重点保护动物等级：二级 亚种 棕尾鵟指名亚种（学名：Buteo rufinus rufinus）。在中国大陆，分布于新疆、甘肃、云南、西藏等地。 参考文献

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|A long-legged buzzard perching in a Picea orientalis|spruce in Turkey. The long-legged buzzard inhabits open, uncultivated areas, with high bushes, trees, cliffs or hillocks are favoured as nesting areas as well as access to freshwater. The species normally lives in steppe, semi-desert and desert edge, barren rocky landscapes, dry shrubland and sometimes sea coasts

links.key: bird-棕尾鵟-40ae5246__distributed_in__loc-熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林-dbaa79b4
links.target: loc-熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林-dbaa79b4
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: 3(1–2): 67–72. While long-legged buzzards predominantly forage in wildlands, they are also adaptable to cultivations, pastures, village outskirts and sometimes even heavily farmed areas. Grasslands are widely used during winter. Those wintering in the Indian subcontinent largely use similar arid open plains, semi-deserts and cultivated areas but perhaps surprisingly are considered a characteristic wintering raptor of dry mixed forests with open glades and barren hill slopes. In the Indian subcontinent, the species may often be seen using a variety of perches including bushes, hedges, Acacia nilotica, sand dunes, haystacks, mounds and power poles. The North African and Arabian race may reportedly show a strong preference for open wooded and/or rocky areas but has been reported in a similarly broad amount of habitats as the nominate subspecies. The species may occur from sea-level up to about in Europe, but in Asia uncommonly lives in mountains at altitudes of up to or even with migrants recorded to . Younger birds disperse north of breeding grounds and there are records from Northern Europe
